channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia’s rivals are focusing on building AI inference chips. Here’s what to know
item.description: Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Trump Meets with Elon Musk: A Closer Look at their Relationship
item.description: President-elect Trump meets with SpaceX CEO Elon Musk, highlighting their close relationship and sparking concerns among Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Germany blames ‘sabotage’ as two undersea fiber cables cut in the Baltic Sea
item.description: The German government has blamed an act of sabotage for the cutting of two important undersea fiber optic cables, one connecting connecting Finland and Germany, and the other linking Sweden and Lithuania, on Sunday and Monday. The first cable break happened at around 8 a.m. GMT (3 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Study: Chat GPT is better than doctors at diagnosing illness
item.description: A new research study indicates that Open AI’s chatbot Chat GPT-4 is better at diagnosing diseases than human doctors, according to The New York Times. Fifty doctors, a mix of attending physicians and residents, participated in the study; diagnoses were based on evaluations of medical patient cases.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Innovations in Robotics: Expanding Human Capabilities
item.description: Discover the latest advancements in robotics, drones, and lidar technology that are expanding human capabilities in public safety, manufacturing, and more.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: The 5 next big things in AR, VR, spatial computing, and multiverse for 2024
item.description: No, the Apple Vision Pro didn’t prove to be the iPhone of AR, but augmented and virtual experiences got a fresh jolt of energy in 2024 all the same.
item.dc:creator: Mark Wilson, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft Aims for Autonomous AI Agents
item.description: Microsoft prepares for a world with AI agents to perform tasks on behalf of businesses
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Linux command line for beginners: 12 essential commands
item.description: Getting started on the Linux command line might seem overwhelming at first, but the many commands you need to use will fall into place more quickly than you might imagine. Let’s start with some introductory but very important commands.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft’s Windows 365 Link is a thin client device for shared workspaces
item.description: Microsoft will start selling a thin client device that lets workers boot directly to Windows 365 “in seconds,” the company announced on Tuesday. Windows 365 Link will cost $349 when it launches next April, but businesses can contact their Microsoft account team and request a private preview.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: A New Hampshire company is working to develop a fleet of autonomous helicopters that it hopes can be used to put out fires, spray crops and handle other dangerous jobs.
item.dc:creator: Michael Casey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Amazon and Elon Musk's SpaceX challenge labor agency's constitutionality in federal court
item.description: Attorneys for Amazon and Elon Musk’s SpaceX have argued in a federal appeals court that the National Labor Relations Board’s structure is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational
item.description: The ETAC has quickly scaled from a pilot project to an operational center at the Energy Department for analyzing cyber threats like Volt Typhoon. The post Energy Department’s ‘ETAC’ cyber threat center goes operational first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Elon Musk's companies balloon in value, with one exception
item.description: Tech giant Elon Musk has successfully expanded several businesses, bolstering his capital and financial prowess to become the richest person on Earth. But the billionaire entrepreneur has seen a sharp decline in the value of the social media platform X which he acquired in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KRON4
